item.title: - Bhikkhus, what matter are you now discussing? What conversation has been interrupted among you? - Here, Venerable Sir, after the meal, returning from the…
item.description:
- Bhikkhus, what matter are you now discussing? What conversation has been interrupted among you?
- Here, Venerable Sir, after the meal, returning from the alms round, we gathered in the assembly hall, competing, disputing, quarrelling, wounding one another with verbal weapons.
- Bhikkhus, it is not proper for you, clansmen who have gone forth out of faith from home to homelessness, to live competing, disputing, quarrelling, wounding one another with verbal weapons. Bhikkhus, there are these ten things that are to be remembered, that create love, create respect, lead to concord, lead to non-dispute, to unity, to harmony. What ten?

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 10.50
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Furthermore, venerable sirs, a bhikkhu is virtuous, living restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest…
item.description:
Furthermore, venerable sirs, a bhikkhu is virtuous, living restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults, undertaking and training in the training rules. Venerable sirs, this is the second support for developing the states pertaining to enlightenment.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 9.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: The door to the Deathless is open, For those who are willing to hear. Renounce your inaccurate faith. Thinking of the trouble, I did not…
item.description:
The door to the Deathless is open,
For those who are willing to hear.
Renounce your inaccurate faith.
Thinking of the trouble,
I did not want to teach
The supreme sublime Dhamma
Among humans.
(O Brahmā)

From: Majjhima Nikāya 26 – Ariyapariyesanāsutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus. A first point is not discerned of beings roaming and wandering on, shrouded by ignorance and fettered…
item.description:
- This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus. A first point is not discerned of beings roaming and wandering on, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving. Bhikkhus, when you see a situation of misery and misfortune, you should conclude: 'We have experienced such for a long time.' Why is that? This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus ... enough to be liberated from all formations.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 15.11
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Just as, bhikkhus, in the autumn, when the sky is clear and cloudless, the sun rises in the sky, dispelling all darkness in space, and…
item.description:
Just as, bhikkhus, in the autumn, when the sky is clear and cloudless, the sun rises in the sky, dispelling all darkness in space, and shines, blazes, and radiates; so too, for the noble disciple, the dust-free, stainless Dhamma-eye arises. And with this arising of knowledge and vision, bhikkhus, three fetters are abandoned: personality view, doubt, and clinging to rites and rituals. Moreover, he is freed from two things: greed and hatred. Secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, he enters and dwells in the first jhāna, which is accompanied by initial and sustained thought, with rapture and happiness born of seclusion. If, bhikkhus, at that time, that noble disciple should die, there is no fetter bound by which he might return to this world.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 3.94
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Defiled by hatred, brahmin, overcome, mind-possessed, one thinks of harming oneself, thinks of harming others, thinks of harming both, and experiences mental suffering and distress.…
item.description:
Defiled by hatred, brahmin, overcome, mind-possessed, one thinks of harming oneself, thinks of harming others, thinks of harming both, and experiences mental suffering and distress. When hatred is abandoned, one does not think of harming oneself, does not think of harming others, does not think of harming both, and does not experience mental suffering and distress. Thus, brahmin, the Dhamma is directly visible, timeless, inviting investigation, onward-leading, to be personally experienced by the wise.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 3.53
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, walk within your own proper range, within the territory of your ancestors. Bhikkhus, if you walk within your own proper range, within the territory…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, walk within your own proper range, within the territory of your ancestors. Bhikkhus, if you walk within your own proper range, within the territory of your ancestors, then long life will increase, beauty will increase, happiness will increase, wealth will increase, power will increase.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 26 – Cakkavatti­sīhanādasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Brahmin, I have practiced for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, I have established many people in the noble truth,…
item.description:
Brahmin, I have practiced for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, I have established many people in the noble truth, namely, in the nature of goodness, the nature of the good Dhamma. I am one who, regarding whatever thought I think should be thought, I think that thought; regarding whatever thought I think should not be thought, I do not think that thought. Regarding whatever intention I intend should be intended, I intend that intention; regarding whatever intention I intend should not be intended, I do not intend that intention. Brahmin, I have attained mastery of mind regarding the directions of thought. Brahmin, regarding the four jhānas that are higher states of mind, pleasant abidings in this very life, I obtain them without difficulty, without trouble, without effort. Brahmin, by the destruction of the taints, in this very life, with direct knowledge, I realize, attain, and abide in the taintless liberation of mind and liberation by wisdom.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.35
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: I do not see any other single factor, bhikkhus, by which unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states are abandoned, as adherence to unwholesome…
item.description:
I do not see any other single factor, bhikkhus, by which unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states are abandoned, as adherence to unwholesome states and non-adherence to wholesome states. Due to adherence to unwholesome states and non-adherence to wholesome states, bhikkhus, unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states are abandoned.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 1.72
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Mind precedes all phenomena, Mind is their master, mind is their maker. If one speaks or acts with a corrupted mind, Suffering follows, Like the…
item.description:
Mind precedes all phenomena,
Mind is their master, mind is their maker.
If one speaks or acts with a corrupted mind,
Suffering follows,
Like the wheel follows the ox's foot.

From: Dhammapada 1-20
📖 Read this sutta
